question.text: The concept of sequence-of-returns risk is most relevant during which phase?

answer.text: The early accumulation phase when the corpus is small
The years just before and just after retirement when the corpus is at its largest
It is not relevant to retirement planning

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Sequence-of-returns risk is most critical near retirement. A market crash when the corpus is at its peak (just before or after retirement) can devastate the portfolio, with insufficient time to recover. **The Trap:** During early accumulation, a market crash actually benefits the investor through lower purchase prices. The risk is concentrated near retirement when the corpus is largest. **Cross-Exam Context:** This risk is why the lifecycle approach recommends reducing equity allocation in the pre-retirement stage — to protect the large accumulated corpus. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Begin systematic de-risking 5-7 years before retirement. Do not wait until the year of retirement to shift from equity to debt.
